perturbation.original_text: The RECEIVING PARTY and its employees, officers, agents and affiliates shall holdthe CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION in confidence and take all reasonable steps to preserve theconfidential and proprietary nature of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, including, withoutlimitation: (i) refraining from disclosing the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to persons within itsorganization not having a reason to know, and all persons outside its organization unless theyhave a reason to know and are bound by fiduciary duties of confidentiality to the RECEIVING PARTY;and (ii) advising all of its employees, officers, agents and affiliates who gain access to theCONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of its confidential and proprietary nature.
perturbation.changed_text: The RECEIVING PARTY and its employees, officers, agents and affiliates shall holdthe CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION in confidence and take steps deemed necessary by the receiving party to preserve theconfidential and proprietary nature of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, including, withoutlimitation: (i) refraining from disclosing the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to persons within itsorganization not having a direct need to know, and all persons outside its organization unless theyhave a direct need to know and are bound by standard confidentiality agreements to the RECEIVING PARTY;and (ii) advising all of its employees, officers, agents and affiliates who gain access to theCONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of its confidential and proprietary nature.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "all reasonable steps" to "steps deemed necessary by the receiving party" and "reason to know" to "direct need to know" introduces ambiguity and weakens the standard of care, potentially conflicting with the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing under Dutch law, which requires parties to act reasonably in fulfilling their contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Dutch Civil Code (Burgerlijk Wetboek) Book 6, Article 2
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 6:2 of the Dutch Civil Code requires parties to act in good faith. The change to 'steps deemed necessary by the receiving party' allows the receiving party to take minimal steps, which may not fulfill the requirement of good faith and reasonable care in protecting confidential information.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: wetten.nl - Regeling - Burgerlijk Wetboek Boek 6 - BWBR0005289 Publicatiepiek op wetten.overheid.nl. Per 1 juli 2025 wordt er een groot aantal regelingen gewijzigd. Mogelijk zijn nog niet alle wijzigingen verwerkt op de datum van inwerkingtreding en ziet u een oude versie van de tekst. Raadpleeg bij twijfel de bekendmaking. perturbation.justification: The modification from 'all reasonable steps' to 'steps deemed necessary by the receiving party' weakens the standard of care required to protect confidential information. This introduces a contradiction by potentially allowing the receiving party to take minimal steps that may not satisfy the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing, which requires reasonable actions. The scraped law snippet refers to Article 6, which relates to verbintenissenrecht (obligations) under Dutch civil code. While the snippet doesn't directly define 'good faith,' the Law Explanation indicates that the altered language could allow for minimal compliance, potentially violating this principle.

perturbation.original_text: In the event the RECEIVING PARTY receives in writing an opinion from itsoutside counsel that it must disclose certain information, prohibited by the terms herein, in orderto avoid committing a violation of law, the RECEIVING PARTY may disclose only that informationnecessary to avoid committing such violation of the law.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event the RECEIVING PARTY receives verbal advice from its legal department that it must disclose certain information, prohibited by the terms herein, in orderto avoid what the legal department believes to be a violation of law, the RECEIVING PARTY may disclose all information they deem necessary.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'written opinion from outside counsel' with 'verbal advice from its legal department' and changing the disclosure scope to 'all information they deem necessary' weakens the protection of confidential information. This contradicts the required standard of seeking qualified, independent legal advice before disclosing information that is protected by the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Dutch Trade Secrets Act (Wet bescherming bedrijfsgeheimen)
perturbation.law_explanation: The Dutch Trade Secrets Act provides protection for trade secrets. Disclosing 'all information they deem necessary' based on internal advice without seeking objective legal counsel undermines the reasonable steps required to maintain the secrecy, potentially negating trade secret protection.
perturbation.justification: The modified contract weakens confidentiality protections by replacing 'written opinion from outside counsel' with 'verbal advice from its legal department' and expanding the disclosure scope to 'all information they deem necessary.' The Law Explanation states this undermines the reasonable steps required to maintain secrecy, potentially negating trade secret protection. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction' definition, where contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions, making the contract unenforceable. Without law snippets, the contradiction is supported by the Perturbation and Law explanations, and the significant change in the disclosure requirements.

perturbation.original_text: All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION supplied by the DISCLOSING PARTY hereunder and all copiesthereof, in whole or in part and on all media, shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY by theRECEIVING PARTY within thirty (30) days upon demand by the DISCLOSING PARTY. All otherinformation and all copies thereof prepared by the RECEIVING PARTY based on the CONFIDENTIALINFORMATION, shall be either promptly destroyed or returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY upondemand, as determined by the DISCLOSING PARTY within its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION supplied by the DISCLOSING PARTY hereunder and all copiesthereof, shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY by theRECEIVING PARTY within ninety (90) days when reasonably possible. All otherinformation, prepared by the RECEIVING PARTY based on the CONFIDENTIALINFORMATION, may be retained as deemed necessary by the RECEIVING PARTY.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be returned within thirty (30) days upon demand' to 'shall be returned within ninety (90) days when reasonably possible' and 'shall be either promptly destroyed or returned' to 'may be retained as deemed necessary' significantly weakens the obligation to return or destroy confidential information. This creates uncertainty and potential violation of intellectual property rights under Dutch law.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Dutch Copyright Act protects the copyright of the disclosing party. By allowing the receiving party to retain copies of derived information as deemed necessary, it potentially infringes on the disclosing party's copyright, as the derived information may be a derivative work protected by copyright.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Internetrecht en technologie uitgelegd door Arnoud Engelfriet (iusmentis.com) Homepage | Categorieën | Lijst A-Z | Willekeurig artikel | Herpubliceren? | Over deze site | Blog | Contact Welkom bij iusmentis.com Internetrecht: Actualiteiten en commentaar De laatste berichten van de Iusmentis blog KPN blokkeert iPhone bij wanbetaling via MDM, mag dat? ier jaar handel ik in iPhones. Ik koop deze o.a. in via Marktplaats en »» u u »» f f »» »» v v »» »» v v »» n n »» a a »» b b »» Lees alle berichten op mijn blog. Met mijn nieuwsbrief krijg je de laatste blogs wekelijks in je mailbox. De wet uitgelegd aan techneuten, en techniek aan juristen. Ius mentis biedt meer dan 350 artikelen over internetrecht en intellectueel eigendom, variërend van regels rond bloggen tot computercriminaliteit . Een uitgebreide introductie is het artikel Mag dat op Internet . De auteur en beheerder is ICT-jurist Arnoud Engelfriet ( blog , contact ). Populairste artikelen De thuiskopie in het Nederlands auteursrecht Een kopie voor strikt eigen gebruik (een "thuiskopie") mag je eigenlijk altijd maken. Echter als het werk van een illegale bron afkomstig is, dan geldt deze regeling niet. Zo'n download is in strijd met het downloadverbod. »» Beschermen van een idee - is vastleggen genoeg? Een idee of concept beschermen is lastig. Octrooi- en auteurswetten eisen een behoorlijke mate van uitwerking of concretisering voordat een concept beschermd kan worden. Het idee opschrijven en ergens deponeren ('vastleggen') biedt meestal maar zeer weinig bescherming. »» Algemene voorwaarden Algemene voorwaarden zijn voorwaarden die bij meerdere overeenkomsten worden gebruikt. Ze zijn ook bindend als de wederpartij ze niet gelezen heeft, maar ze mogen niet onredelijk bezwarend zijn. »» Colportage op internet: de Wet Koop op Afstand De wet Koop op Afstand beschermt de consument door bindende regels bij koop via Internet. De belangrijkste zijn het recht op ontbinding van de koop binnen 14 dagen en de informatieplicht voor de verkoper. »» Populairste onderwerpen Octrooien Software , Europa , Inventiviteit , Spoedcursus , FAQ (Engelstalig) Websites en blogs Legaal bloggen , Webdesign , Hyperlinks , MP3-sites , Zoekmachines Auteursrecht MP3 , File sharing , Foto's , Spoedcursus , Thuiskopie Beveiliging Virussen , Computercriminaliteit , E-mail , Wachtwoorden , Spyware Merken Spoedcursus (Engelstalig) , Domeinnamen , UDRP Internetten File sharing , Providers , Privacy , Op het werk , Meningsuiting Databankrecht Spoedcursus (Engelstalig) , In de VS (Engelstalig) , In Nederland Technologie File sharing , Encryptie , Digitale handtekeningen , TCP/IP (Engelstalig) Innovatie Uitvinden , Idee vastleggen , Open innovatie Software Open source , Licenties , Octrooien Intellectuele eigendom Wat is IE/IPR , Spoedcursussen (Engelstalig) Zakendoen Contracten , E-commerce , Elektronisch betalen , Wet Koop op Afstand Zie alle categorieën of alle artikelen . perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates the return or destruction of confidential information and related copies. The changed text weakens this obligation by extending the return timeframe and allowing the receiving party to retain copies as deemed necessary. This contradicts Dutch copyright law, which protects the copyright of the disclosing party, as derived information may be a derivative work protected by copyright, as explained by the LLM. The changed text could infringe on the disclosing party's copyright if the receiving party retains copies and uses them in a way that exploits the copyright owner's exclusive rights, as the LLM suggests. While the scraped snippets don't provide direct evidence for copyright infringement, the LLM explanation, combined with the weakening of the return/destroy obligation, establishes a significant contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that may have beenprovided to the RECEIVING PARTY prior to or after the date hereof, and shall continue to govern thedelivery of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION until terminated by written notice from either party to theother, except that the obligations of the parties hereunder with regard to CONFIDENTIALINFORMATION disclosed prior to termination shall continue for a period for two (2) years thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that may have beenprovided to the RECEIVING PARTY prior to or after the date hereof, and shall continue to govern thedelivery of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION until terminated by either party with reasonable cause. The obligations of the parties hereunder with regard to CONFIDENTIALINFORMATION disclosed prior to termination shall cease immediately upon termination.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the fixed two-year obligation after termination and replacing it with immediate cessation upon termination alters the protection period for confidential information. This could contradict the expectation of continued confidentiality and fair competition, which is implicitly protected under Dutch unfair competition laws.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Dutch Act on Unfair Competition (Wet oneerlijke mededinging)
perturbation.law_explanation: The Dutch Act on Unfair Competition protects against actions that unfairly harm competitors. By immediately ceasing confidentiality obligations, the receiving party could potentially use confidential information to unfairly compete with the disclosing party immediately after termination, which could be considered unfair competition.

perturbation.justification: The original contract ensures confidentiality for two years post-termination, while the changed contract eliminates this protection, causing immediate cessation of confidentiality obligations. The Law Explanation indicates that this immediate cessation could allow the receiving party to unfairly compete using confidential information, violating Dutch unfair competition laws. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 2 states that general conditions in contracts 'may not be unreasonably onerous,' which could be argued in court if the immediate cessation of confidentiality creates a scenario where the original contract terms are unreasonably onerous.

perturbation.original_text: In case of unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION,The DISCLOSING PARTY shall be entitled to liquidated damages in the amount of €10 000 (tenthousand Euro) for each such use or disclosure. Notwithstanding the right to liquidated damages,the DISCLOSING PARTY has the right to take any measures available and to claim and receivea higher amount of compensation if the DISCLOSING PARTY can prove that the actual damagesustained will exceed the amount of liquidated damages.
perturbation.changed_text: In case of unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION,The DISCLOSING PARTY shall be entitled to liquidated damages in the amount of €100 (onehundred Euro) for each such use or disclosure. The DISCLOSING PARTY waives the right to take any measures available and cannot claim and receivea higher amount of compensation even if the DISCLOSING PARTY can prove that the actual damagesustained will exceed the amount of liquidated damages.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the liquidated damages amount from €10,000 to €100 and waiving the right to claim higher compensation restricts the remedies available to the disclosing party. This could be deemed unconscionable or unreasonable, especially if actual damages are significantly higher, and might not be enforceable under Dutch contract law principles regarding reasonableness and fairness.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Dutch Civil Code (Burgerlijk Wetboek) Book 6, Article 248
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 6:248 of the Dutch Civil Code allows a court to set aside contractual terms that are unreasonably onerous or unfair. The drastically reduced liquidated damages and the waiver of the right to claim higher compensation could be considered unreasonable, especially if the actual damages suffered by the disclosing party are substantially greater.

Geldend van 01-01-1992 t/m heden Toon relaties in LiDO Maak een permanente link Toon wetstechnische informatie Geen andere versie om mee te vergelijken Druk de regeling af Sla de regeling op Origineel opschrift en aanhef Wet van 9 oktober 1991, tot wijziging van de Wet uitkeringen vervolgingsslachtoffers 1940-1945 en de Wet uitkeringen burger-oorlogsslachtoffers 1940-1945 (invoering gelijke behandeling van mannen en vrouwen alsmede enige andere wijzigingen) Wij Beatrix, bij de gratie Gods, Koningin der Nederlanden, Prinses van Oranje-Nassau, enz. enz. enz. Allen, die deze zullen zien of horen lezen, saluut! doen te weten: Alzo Wij in overweging genomen hebben, dat het wenselijk is gelijke behandeling van mannen en vrouwen in te voeren in de Wet uitkeringen vervolgingsslachtoffers 1940-1945 ( Stb. 1986, 386) en de Wet uitkeringen burger-oorlogsslachtoffers 1940-1945 ( Stb. 1984, 94), alsmede enige andere wijzigingen in die wetten aan te brengen; Zo is het, dat Wij, de Raad van State gehoord, en met gemeen overleg der Staten-Generaal, hebben goedgevonden en verstaan, gelijk Wij goedvinden en verstaan bij deze: Artikel I Toon relaties in LiDO Maak een permanente link Toon wetstechnische informatie Geen andere versie om mee te vergelijken Druk het regelingonderdeel af Sla het regelingonderdeel op [Red: Bevat wijzigingen in andere regelgeving.] Artikel II Toon relaties in LiDO Maak een permanente link Toon wetstechnische informatie Geen andere versie om mee te vergelijken Druk het regelingonderdeel af Sla het regelingonderdeel op [Red: Bevat wijzigingen in andere regelgeving.] Artikel III Toon relaties in LiDO Maak een permanente link Toon wetstechnische informatie Geen andere versie om mee te vergelijken Druk het regelingonderdeel af Sla het regelingonderdeel op - 1. Bij de inwerkingtreding van deze wet reeds toegekende rechten ingevolge de Wet uitkeringen vervolgingsslachtoffers 1940-1945 of de Wet uitkeringen burger-oorlogsslachtoffers 1940-1945 worden bij de inwerkingtreding van deze wet niet opnieuw getoetst aan de artikelen van deze wetten zoals deze dan komen te luiden behoudens voor zover het betreft de artikelen 17 en 19 van de Wet uitkeringen vervolgingsslachtoffers 1940-1945 en 23 en 28 van de Wet uitkeringen burger-oorlogsslachtoffers 1940-1945 . Indien ingevolge de vorige volzin de artikelen 17 en 19
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly reduces the liquidated damages amount and waives the right to claim higher compensation, potentially contradicting Dutch contract law principles regarding reasonableness and fairness, as explained by the LLM and the perturbation explanation. While the scraped law snippet is irrelevant, the core argument that this waiver could be deemed unconscionable remains valid based on the other provided information.
